channel.title: The Easier Now blog
channel.description: Writing on getting work done without holding it all in your head.

item.title: Time estimates are a skill, not a personality trait
item.description: People who are bad at guessing how long work takes usually think they were born that way. It is a skill with a feedback loop, and most people have never closed the loop.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Stratton

item.title: Why delegation fails when the process only exists in your head
item.description: Handing work over rarely fails because the person is wrong. It fails because the instructions were never written down, and nobody can read your mind well enough to fill the gaps.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Stratton

item.title: What stoicism gets right about the next action
item.description: The Stoics split the world into what you control and what you do not. Applied to a working day, that line falls in a more useful place than most people expect.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Stratton
